data.title: Amazon sellers will pass tariff costs on to customers: CEO
data.description:
Andy Jassy, CEO of retail giant Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), has said its third-party sellers may “past costs on” related to US President Donald Trump’s tariffs to customers.

Jassy made the remarks in an interview on CNBC on April 10, noting that the company was still evaluating the impact of Trump’s tariffs.

“I understand why, I mean, depending on which country you’re in, you don’t have 50% extra margin that you can play with,” Jassy said in the interview, explaining why sellers would pass on the costs.

Trump most recently issued a 90-day pause on his “reciprocal” tariffs but retained the 10% base rate and ratcheted up levies against China to 125%. Tariffs on steel and aluminum imports also remain at 25%.

The head of Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) also said the company has made strategic inventory purchases and is moving to renegotiate certain purchase orders to mitigate the impact of tariffs.

data.title: What if Apple doesn't raise like-for-like iPhone prices? Morgan Stanley answers
data.description:
Morgan Stanley expects Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) to significantly minimize the tariff headwind and mitigate 125% China levies, with fast-ramping India production and targeted iPhone mix shift.

Since tariffs on Chinese imports were first introduced in early March, the expectation has been that Apple will be forced to raise iPhone prices to offset the consistently increasing cost of tariffs on Chinese imports.

Recent production data and news reports also support that Apple has pulled forward inventory and redirected some production from India to minimize the impact of tariffs on U.S.-bound products.

However, Morgan Stanley doesn't believe Apple is solely betting on a presidential exemption (potential company-specific tariff carve-outs) and the following steps could be a less disruptive path that Apple could take: 

  • With the 90-day reciprocal tariff reprieve, Apple will look to its ODM partners in India – Hon Hai, Pegatron, and Tata – and communicate the desire to significantly ramp production in India to help meet US iPhone demand.
  • Gross margin on the highest-end iPhone storage SKU is 10-15 points higher than the lowest storage SKU, and therefore, if Apple can shift demand towards higher-margin iPhone models, it can lessen the blow from China tariffs as India production also ramps.
  • At the same time, Apple could leverage financing plans, and higher carrier subsidies/trade-in values to help offset the synthetically higher iPhone prices.
  • What if Apple extended the Apple Card iPhone installment plan to 36 months? For a $1,099 iPhone, that would cut the monthly iPhone payment 33%, from $45 to $30, and also drive more users to the Apple Card.

To conclude, with the largest base of pent-up iPhone demand ever, new AI features rolling out (slowly) around the world, and investments in cloud, and payments, the Bank believes Apple can accelerate iPhone growth starting in FY27.

data.title: Newsmax falsely reported on Dominion's involvement in 2020 election rigging - Delaware Judge
data.description:
A judge in Delaware ruled that conservative network Newsmax Media (NYSE:NMAX) defamed and made false statements against Dominion Voting Systems by alleging the electronic voting hardware and software firm was involved in rigging the 2020 presidential election, which Donald Trump lost to Joe Biden.

Delaware Superior Court Judge Eric Davis is still on the fence whether the news outlet has to pay damages for the defamation in the $1.6B suit filed by Dominion.

"Newsmax covered both sides of the 2020 election dispute fairly. At no time did it defame Dominion. This case represents a serious threat to free speech and a free press, and Newsmax will defend itself vigorously at trial,” a spokesperson for the pro-Trump news company said Wednesday.

A representative for the voting company said, "We are gratified by the court’s thorough ruling."

The possibility of a settlement outside court is still on the table. In 2023, Dominion won a similar lawsuit against Fox (NASDAQ:FOXA) and got $787M in damages.

Shares of Newsmax have seen volatile surges and declines since going public on March 28. NMAX closed down 11.5% on Wednesday and is down 2% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: UK antitrust regulator may accept SLB-ChampionX remedies
data.description:
The UK's Competition and Markets Authority said it may accept the remedies offered by SLB (NYSE:SLB) and oilfield services company ChampionX  (NASDAQ:CHX) for their planned deal. 

The CMA said that there are "reasonable grounds" to believe that the undertakings offered by SLB and ChampionX (NASDAQ:CHX) may be accepted by the CMA under the Enterprise ACT 2002, according to a statement the regulator made on Thursday.

SLB has offered to sell its chemical technologies business in the UK and ChampionX (CHX) said in February that it will sell its equity interests in US Synthetic. 

The CMA has until June 11 to make a final decision on the deal, though it can be extended until Aug. 8. 

SLB, formerly Schlumberger, announced last April that it agreed to acquire ChampionX (CHX) in an all-stock deal, saying it will strengthen its leadership position in oilfield services with world-class production chemicals and artificial lift technologies.

data.title: Neptune Digital Assets expands Bitcoin treasury to over 400 BTC
data.description:
• Neptune Digital Assets (OTCQB:NPPTF) on Thursday announced that it has expanded its Bitcoin treasury to 401 BTC.
  • Neptune's average acquisition price per Bitcoin is $31,564.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tetra Tech awarded £36M contract for water infrastructure services
data.description:
• Tetra Tech (NASDAQ:TTEK) said on Thursday that Severn Trent Water has selected Tetra Tech for a £36M multiple-award contract to provide comprehensive water infrastructure services in the United Kingdom.
  • Under the 3-year contract, Tetra Tech engineers will conduct geotechnical investigations to assess soil and groundwater conditions to support the development of Severn Trent Water’s capital improvements projects. 
  • Tetra Tech (NASDAQ:TTEK) shares were down 1.57% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Amazon CEO talks about AI investment, Kuiper and operating like 'world’s largest startup' in letter to shareholders
data.description:
Amazon's (NASDAQ:AMZN) CEO Andy Jassy said in his annual letter to shareholders that Amazon is aiming to be the "Earth’s most customer-centric company," while highlighting AI infrastructure and services that the company has introduced and working on.

Jassy said that the company strives to "operate like the world’s largest startup," meaning that whatever it is contemplating building has to be focused on solving a real customer problem or improving customer experience.

He added that companies can get off track prioritizing technology because they’re excited about the technology. "Great startups are on a mission to change what’s possible for customers," said Jassy.

Artificial Intelligence

On AI, Jassy said that, "generative AI is going to reinvent virtually every customer experience we know, and enable altogether new ones about which we’ve only fantasized."

Jassy noted that if a company's customer experiences are not planning to use these intelligent models, their ability to get smarter with feedback and data, and future agentic capabilities, they will not be competitive. "How soon? It won’t all happen in a year or two, but, it won’t take ten either. It’s moving faster than almost anything technology has ever seen," Jassy added.

On the topic of why to invest in AI and "this much this quickly," Jassy said that if one believes every customer experience will be reinvented by AI, then they are going to invest deeply and broadly in AI.

Jassy added that there are more than 1,000 GenAI applications being built across Amazon, aiming to change customer experiences in areas including shopping, coding, personal assistants, streaming video and music, advertising, healthcare, reading, and home devices.

"It’s also why AWS is quickly developing the key primitives (or building blocks) for AI development, such as custom silicon AI chips in Amazon Trainium to provide better price-performance on training and inference, highly flexible model-building and inference services in Amazon SageMaker and Amazon Bedrock, our own frontier models in Amazon Nova to provide lower cost and latency for customers’ applications, and agent creation and management capabilities" Jassy wrote in the letter.

Capital Investment in AI

The CEO noted that "substantial capital investment" is required. In Amazon Web Services, or AWS, the faster demand grows, the more datacenters, chips, and hardware the company needs to procure, according to Jassy. He added that the company spends this capital upfront, even though these assets are useful for many years, in the case of datacenters, for at least 15 to 20 years.

He added that the company starts monetizing this capital investment many months after its spends the capital, and over many years, which leads to attractive long-term Free cash flow, or FCF, and Return On Invested Capital, or ROIC. 

Jassy blamed chips to be "biggest culprit" for why AI is expensive today but added that AI won’t be so expensive in the future. He noted that the company's new Trainium2 chips offer 30% to 40% better price-performance than the current graphics processing unit, or GPU-powered compute instances generally available today. 

Alexa+

Jassy also touted Alexa+, which he said, "is not only comparably intelligent to the leading chatbots, but can take a plethora of real actions for you."

Project Kuiper

In addition, Jassy talked about Project Kuiper, the company's low Earth orbit satellite network, which would help millions of people without broadband connectivity access internet.

He said the company is launching its first production satellites, and will ultimately have over 3,200 in orbit over the next few years. "While capital-intensive to launch, we believe Kuiper will be a meaningful operating income and ROIC business for us," Jassy added.

data.title: CBD Life Sciences online sales jump 500%
Harley-Davidson director calls out 'cultural depletion' of brand
data.description:
In the wake of CEO Jochen Zeitz’s retirement announcement, Harley-Davidson (NYSE:HOG) board member Jared Dourdeville raised serious concerns about the company’s management and decisions that have damaged the Harley-Davidson (NYSE:HOG) brand.

According to a redacted letter disclosed through an SEC filing, Dourdeville, who represents second-largest shareholder H. Partners, singled out Zeitz for the company’s failed strategies, and had previously called for his immediate resignation, as well as those from lead independent director Tom Lineburger, and board member Sara Levinson.

Dourdeville’s decision to leave the board was predicated on decisions by Zeitz and the board that resulted in the “cultural depletion of Harley-Davidson” which includes the “decimation at the corporate level which has become more glaring in recent months.”

Dourdeville also became more concerned about the lack of accountability and transparency, crisis in the company’s dealer network, specifically the decision to increase inventory levels and carrying costs which “padded Harley-Davidson’s profits” at the expense of dealer profitability.

Additionally, Harley-Davidson’s (NYSE:HOG) response to the Robby Starbuck DEI campaign was “grossly mismanaged” resulting in a “material and lasting impact on customers’ relationship with the Harley-Davidson brand.”

While Dourdeville still believes in the company’s long-term potential, he cautions that H Partners reserves the right to take any action deemed necessary to protect its shareholders.

Harley-Davidson (HOG) shares are down ~3% in premarket trading.

data.title: Wellgistics Health to acquire Peek Healthcare Technologies
data.description:
• Wellgistics Health (NASDAQ:WGRX) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Peek Healthcare to expand its digital healthcare ecosystem.
  • Upon completion of the transaction, Peek will operate as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Wellgistics Health, maintaining its brand identity while gaining access to an expanded infrastructure, technology resources, and strategic pharmacy relationships.
  • WGRX +5.58% premarket to $4.54.
  • Source: Press Release
